content: There are only 182 Black students at Cal Poly, making up 0.8% of the total population, according to 2025 data. In 1995, Black students made up 1.9% of the population, more than double the current percentage. What’s more, the actual number of Black students has decreased, even as general enrollment grew steadily for the past 30 years. While enrollment of Hispanic students jumped from 12% to 28% in that same 30-year time period and Cal Poly’s Asian population increased from 8% to 13%, white students still dominate campus demographics to this day, making up 46% in 2025.
